summary: I was born in Bangkok, Thailand, and it holds a special place in my heart as my hometown. Growing up there shaped my early years and my identity before I moved away. Leaving Bangkok at thirteen was tough because I had to say goodbye to my friends and family who were an important part of my life. Even though I live far away now, Bangkok and the memories I have there continue to influence who I am.

Thailand is where I spent my childhood and where my family and friends still live. Moving from Thailand to the United States at thirteen was a major life change for me. Despite the challenges, my connection to Thailand remains strong, and I often remind myself that no matter how much I change, I will always be Thai. My family and friends in Thailand have been a great support during my transition to a new culture.

I moved to the United States when I was thirteen, which was a difficult and transformative time in my life. Coming here meant I had to adjust everything about how I communicate, eat, and dress to fit in with American culture. Although it was challenging to leave my old life behind, I have managed to blend into the American way of life while still holding on to my Thai identity. The United States became my new home where I learned to navigate a different culture and environment.
